summary: The permanent second molar tends to move towards the mesial direction after losing its adjacent primary or permanent first molar; however, individual variations and other factors can influence this outcome differently based on specific patient conditions observed clinically over various studies reviewed here. Drifting patterns may vary depending upon additional oral health dynamics present within each unique case context beyond just simple adjacency effects alone suggested above generally speaking during
